# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Kong configuration for API routes that proxies to microservices. Configure upstream services, routes, and plugins for a production API gateway.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent api gateway security.
# Category: api_gateway_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: kong-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: microservicesApp   # Selects the pod with this label in namespace default (if not specified, defaults to current context)
---    
type: LoadBalancer       # ExternalIP or NodePort can be used as a load balancing method. Default is None which means no external IP for service ‘kong-service’ and it will use the internal Kong ingress controller's DNS name (if available).  
ports:                   # Define ports to expose your microservices on this port number in kubernetes cluster, you can define multiple as per requirement. For example if we have two services running at different places then they should be exposed over these 2 Ports too and Kong will handle the routing between them based upon request path or host header
 - name: http-port   # Name of port (optional) for external access to your microservices, defaults is 'http' in kong. If you want a different service then use that as well if required by API Gateway else leave it blank 
     protocol: TCP    # Protocol type can be HTTP/HTTPS or any other supported one like TLS etc (optional) and default value will always be either 'HTTP' for Kong ingress controller. If you want to expose your microservices over HTTPS then use that as well if required by API Gateway else leave it blank
     port: 80          # Port number on which the service should listen, defaults is any available TCP ports (optional) and default value will be 'http' in Kong. If you want a different external access to your microservices than HTTP then use that as well if required by API Gateway else leave it blank
     targetPort: 80    # Port number on which the service should listen, defaults is any available TCP ports (optional) and default value will be 'http' in Kong. If you want a different external access to your microservices than HTTP then use that as well if required by API Gateway else leave it blank
---    
apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1  # Define the plugins for routing requests between services (optional) and default is none which means no plugin will be used. For example, 'request_ratelimit', etc can also be added as per requirement if required by API Gateway else leave it blank  
plugins:                    # List of Plugins to use in Kong configuration file – optional but recommended (optional) and default is none which means no plugin will be used. For example, 'request_ratelimit', etc can also be added as per requirement if required by API Gateway else leave it blank
